questions.prompt: Which action best reflects active reading?

choices.text: Reading quickly without stopping so you can finish sooner
choices.feedback: Active reading is deliberate and requires engagement while reading, not rushing through the text.

choices.text: Annotating important ideas and asking questions as you read
choices.feedback: Correct. Active reading involves tracking thinking through actions like annotation and questioning.

choices.text: Waiting until the end to think about the text
choices.feedback: Active reading happens during reading, not only after the text is finished.

choices.text: Copying every sentence into your notes
choices.feedback: Copying everything does not show purposeful attention to meaning.

questions.prompt: What is the central idea of a text?

choices.text: A detail that appears in one paragraph
choices.feedback: A central idea is broader than one detail and is supported across the text.

choices.text: The author's exact first sentence
choices.feedback: The central idea may be introduced early, but it is not simply the first sentence.

choices.text: What the text is mostly saying overall
choices.feedback: Correct. The central idea is the main message developed throughout the text.

choices.text: A reader's favorite part of the passage
choices.feedback: Personal preference does not determine the central idea.

questions.prompt: Which detail would best support the central idea that a school reading program helps students improve?

choices.text: The program meets in the library after lunch
choices.feedback: This detail gives location and time, but it does not clearly support improvement.

choices.text: Students in the program showed stronger comprehension scores by the end of the semester
choices.feedback: Correct. This detail directly supports the idea that the program helps students improve.

choices.text: The teacher leading the program likes historical fiction
choices.feedback: This is unrelated to whether the program improves student reading.

choices.text: Some students wear backpacks with the school logo
choices.feedback: This does not connect to the central idea of reading improvement.

questions.prompt: In the sentence "The trail was steep and rocky, so the climb was strenuous," what does strenuous most likely mean?

choices.text: Easy and relaxing
choices.feedback: The clues steep and rocky suggest the opposite of easy.

choices.text: Slow and quiet
choices.feedback: The context emphasizes difficulty, not silence or pace.

choices.text: Difficult and requiring effort
choices.feedback: Correct. The context clues show that strenuous means hard and effortful.

choices.text: Short and unimportant
choices.feedback: The surrounding details do not suggest length or importance.

questions.prompt: A text's structure can influence how a reader understands the relationship among ideas.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Structure shapes meaning by organizing how ideas connect.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Text structure does affect meaning because organization helps readers interpret ideas.

questions.prompt: Which statement best fits an objective summary?

choices.text: It restates the main idea and key points without opinion
choices.feedback: Correct. An objective summary reports the text's meaning without personal judgment.

choices.text: It explains whether the reader liked the text
choices.feedback: Personal reactions make a summary less objective.

choices.text: It lists every detail from the text in order
choices.feedback: A summary should be selective rather than list every detail.

choices.text: It focuses only on the most surprising sentence
choices.feedback: An objective summary should represent the whole text, not one sentence.

questions.prompt: If a reader notices that a paragraph suddenly stops making sense, what is the best next step?

choices.text: Keep reading without slowing down
choices.feedback: Monitoring comprehension requires noticing confusion and responding to it.

choices.text: Reread and choose a strategy to repair understanding
choices.feedback: Correct. Skilled readers notice confusion and apply a fix-up strategy.

choices.text: Assume the paragraph is not important
choices.feedback: Ignoring confusion does not improve comprehension.

choices.text: Skip to the end and summarize from memory
choices.feedback: A reader should first repair understanding before moving on.

questions.prompt: A strong reading routine can combine several reading skills instead of using each skill separately.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. An integrated reading routine combines skills so a reader can understand a text more effectively.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: A reading routine is meant to bring together multiple useful reading skills.
